trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 5 eval returned exprate=0.0 (only 2 samples within edit distance 2). Model barely better than random. Analysis: The ResNet-50 encoder with stride=32 loses fine details (handwritten characters ~40px height become 1-2 feature pixels). No 2D positional encoding on flattened image features breaks spatial structure. Also missing memory padding mask may cause attention to padding. Decision: Upgrade encoder to ResNet-50 up to layer3 (stride=16) for higher resolution, add 2D sinusoidal positional encoding, add memory_key_padding_mask, increase epochs to 20 with better augmentation and learning rate schedule, implement beam search. Submit eval after training to measure improvement.

Must also fix model encode method, ensure predict.py uses correct shape, and train longer with better schedule. Decision: rewrite all three files with fixes: remove verbose, add model.encode(), proper memory transpose, better LR schedule (warmup+cosine), mixed precision, 30 epochs, beam width 5. Submit eval after training to measure improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: iter 7 training crashed due to memory_key_padding_mask size mismatch (got 1340, expected 1360). The mask computed from input image dimensions does not match the encoder's actual output sequence length because padding/cropping changes. Analysis: The memory mask alignment is fragile and not essential for initial experiments; we can disable it (pass None). The improved architecture (stride=16, 2D pos enc, beam search) hasn't been trained yet; we need a working training run to obtain a non-zero baseline. Decision: Fix train.py to skip memory mask, reduce max image size to max_h=128, max_w=512 to keep sequence length manageable (~256), add mixed precision and proper scheduler. Train 10 epochs, then submit eval to measure improvement over zero exprate.

trajectory.description: Observation: Previous eval exprate=0.0, val loss stagnating ~2.8-2.9 showing overfitting. Encoder stride 16 might lose fine details; need finer features and regularization. Analysis: Can improve encoder to stride 8 for higher resolution (40px strokes become ~5px feature pixels) while using adaptive pooling to keep sequence length ~256. Add label smoothing (0.1), stronger augmentation (elastic), increased dropout, weight decay. Train longer (30 epochs) to see if generalization improves. Decision: Rewrite model.py with ResNet stride-8 + adaptive pooling, update train.py with better regularization and logging, then train 30 epochs and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: iter 10 eval (iter 9's model) exprate=0.0, exprate_le2=0.0083. Stride-8 ResNet encoder, 4-layer decoder, 30 epochs gave train loss 2.1 but val loss 3.13 and zero exact match. Analysis: model too weak to capture HMER patterns; need higher resolution encoder (stride 4), stronger decoder (6 layers, d_model=512), DenseNet-121 backbone for better feature reuse, and image inversion (white strokes) to align with pretrained weights. Decision: build new model with DenseNet-121 stride-4 encoder, adaptive pool to (16,64), 6-layer decoder (d_model=512, ff=2048, nhead=8), label smoothing 0.1, invert images. Run 5 epochs quick test (expected <15 min) and submit eval to measure improvement before scaling up.

trajectory.description: Observation: iter 11 command failed with RuntimeError: attn_mask shape [29,29] should be (8,8). Analysis: mask computed from tgt length 29, but self-attention received query length 8, indicating a mismatch in how the mask was generated relative to the actual target passed (likely a test forward). Decision: fix by computing tgt_mask inside model.forward (auto-mask generation) to guarantee consistency, remove any pre-training test forward, adjust encoder to pool_grid=(8,32) for manageable sequence length 256, and add label smoothing, AMP, warmup+cosine schedule, and gradient clipping. Train for 30 epochs with DenseNet stride-4 encoder and 6-layer decoder, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: iter 12 training completed (30 epochs, ~1.43h). Train loss 1.32, val loss 3.49, severe overfitting. All previous eval exprate=0.0, exprate_le2=0.008. Analysis: training data preprocessing likely flawed — collate crops images that exceed batch max dimensions, losing content (e.g., tall images cropped top/bottom). This explains poor generalization. Also encoder (DenseNet block1 only) is shallow but may suffice with correct data. Decision: fix dataset to resize images preserving aspect ratio (no cropping), add label smoothing (0.1), increase dropout to 0.3, train 15 epochs (est. ~0.7h) with fixed preprocessing, then submit eval to measure improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: iter 13 eval exprate=0.0, exprate_le2=0.0, despite 15 epochs of training with preprocessing fixes. Previous models also zero exact match. Analysis: model may be generating gibberish or failing to learn meaningful token sequences. The training loss 4.6 is high (close to random log 4.9), indicating weak learning. Need to debug pipeline: check if beam search outputs sensible tokens on training data, verify teacher forcing and shift in training loop, consider deeper encoder and better decoder setup. Decision: write a debug script to run inference on a few training samples and print predicted vs. ground truth tokens, to diagnose prediction quality. Then based on findings, overhaul architecture (full DenseNet, correct training loop).

trajectory.description: Observation: Predictions are all 'R' tokens even on training data, despite training loss ~1.32. This indicates either the checkpoint is not loaded correctly, the encoder provides no useful signal, or the preprocessing destroys features. Need diagnosis to understand root cause before further training. Analysis: Possible causes: (1) Inversion of images may harm pretrained features; (2) State dict mismatch; (3) Model ignoring memory; (4) Beam search bug. Decision: Write and run a diagnostic script to inspect vocab mapping, checkpoint loading, encoder output statistics, and first-token logit distributions on real vs noise inputs. Based on findings, next iteration will apply targeted fix and retrain.

trajectory.description: Observation: Diagnostic shows encoder output nearly indistinguishable from noise; decoder defaults to unigram LM (all 'R'). This explains all prior zero exprate results. Analysis: Encoder too shallow (only DenseNet block1) and image inversion likely corrupts pretrained feature alignment. Need deeper encoder (DenseNet up to denseblock3, stride 16) and no inversion. Decision: Upgrade encoder, remove image inversion, adjust max image size (192×768), train 5 epochs (~20 min) to quickly test if any non-zero exprate emerges. This is a critical last attempt given only 0.6 h remaining.
